Walter Cueva

• 3rd+

Helping businesses build, scale & monetize tech | CTO | Founder | AI, Web & Product Strategy | Let’s build your next move

1d •

A young crypto streamer in a green hoodie has become the face of a viral security warning after a devastating "rookie mistake" was captured in real-time. This video shows the exact moment the creator accidentally flashed a plain-text note containing his 24-word seed phrase, triggering an immediate feeding frenzy by automated OCR bots that monitor live broadcasts. While some reports exaggerated the loss, community data confirms approximately $100,000 in SOL and digital assets were drained from his Phantom wallet in under 30 seconds, well before he could even switch windows. You won’t believe the look of pure shock as he realizes his "life savings" were imported and emptied by anonymous scripts while thousands watched the disaster unfold. See the full breakdown of how these bots strike in milliseconds, link bio. Or comment JOE to follow updates on this story and join our community awareness newsletter, always free. credit: @thekindjoe / Instagram

        Edu Pharma Community Founder | Claude AI enthusiast | Community Pharmacist |

        1d • Edited •

        Pacijent je u petak došao sa pojačanom terapijom za dijabetes. Stara terapija: →vildagliptin →glimepirid 3 mg Nova terapija: →vildagliptin →glimepirid 2 mg → metformin 500 mg 2x1 Šećer joj se kretao između 16-19 mmol/L sa starom terapijom. Nije dobila uputu za dijabetologa/endokrinologa, iako znam da joj je vildagliptin uveden prije cca. 3 mjeseca. Zovem ljekara: →ŠUK je previsok da biste smanjili glimepirid →ŠUK je previsok da bi dobila samo metformin 2*500 mg →hitna kontrola AST/ALT →hitno dijabetologu/endokrinologu Ljekar ispravio glimepirid i napisao uputnicu →jer nažalost samo to može. A ja? Niko me nije pohvalio. Ni ljekar. Ni menadžment. I to je u redu. Ali trebate znati: → svaki dan farmaceuti u ovoj regiji rade upravo ovo. Tiho. Bez fanfare. Farmaceut nije šalter. Farmaceut je sigurnosna mreža zdravstvenog sistema. A dok god sistem to ne prepozna → mi ćemo i dalje raditi isto. Jer pacijent to zaslužuje. P.S. Imaš sličnu priču iz prakse? Napiši je u komentarima. Hajde da pokažemo koliko toga radimo tiho, svaki dan.

        Usama Abid

        • 3rd+

        AI Engineer | Building Intelligent Cloud Systems for 40% Faster Deployments & 30% Cost Reduction | DevOps • MLOps • Kubernetes • AWS • Python

        3d •

        This screenshot says more than a thousand words.

        An AI agent was told:
        “Don’t write outside your workspace.”

        It agreed…
        Then found another way to do it anyway.

        Not malicious. Just… creative.

        And that’s exactly why DevOps fundamentals matter in AI

        Without proper isolation:
        - You’re trusting the agent
        - You’re hoping it behaves
        - You’re exposed to unexpected risks

        With containers (Docker, etc.):
        - You enforce boundaries
        - You control execution
        - You limit damage
        - You make systems predictable

        Key lesson:
        AI doesn’t just follow rules.
        It explores possibilities.

        If there’s a gap between “allowed” and “possible”…
        It will find it.

        That’s why I always tell my students:
        Don’t just learn AI tools.
        Learn systems, security, and DevOps basics.

        Because real-world AI = AI + infrastructure + control.
        If you want to learn this the right way (hands-on, real-world):
        DM “DEVOPS” for 1:1 mentoring.

        #ai #devops #docker #security

        Sunny Bakshi

        • 2nd

        Recruitment Director | Global Search & Senior Hiring | FTSE 100, Fortune 500, PE-backed & Growth Organisations (LATAM, North America, Europe, APAc & Emerging Markets)

        Visit my website

        3h • Edited •

        50km ultra run at Windsor done. Ultra Challenge®

        This one wasn't really just about running — it was about endurance.

        The last couple of years in business have been tough. The hardest since we started.
        Pressure, big decisions, uncertainty — the kind that tests you properly.

        Everyone talks about running a business like it's exciting.
        Reality is, it's a grind. You take hits, you keep going, and you learn quickly that not everyone lasts the pace.

        The last 7 months though — things have turned.
        With Rachel, we've steadied it, made better calls, and just closed our most profitable year in 8 years.

        No magic. Just resilience, discipline, and sticking at it when it would've been easier not to.

        That's exactly what the 50km felt like.
        Long stretches where nothing feels good. Legs heavy, mind drifting, plenty of reasons to slow down or stop.
        But you don't. You just keep moving forward.

        I've realised that mindset didn't come from nowhere.
        I got it from my parents — coming over from India, working through tough conditions, and showing us what resilience actually looks like. No shortcuts, no excuses.

        Finishing it with my daughters Maya & Eila and my wife Rachel by my side — easily the best part.

        On we go. 100 miler next.

        #endurance #entrepreneurship #resilience #ultrachallenge

          Eduardo Ordax

          • 3rd+

          🤖 Generative AI Lead @ AWS ☁️ (200k+) | Startup Advisor | Public Speaker | AI Outsider | Founder Thinkfluencer AI

          1d •

          More papers like this, please 🙏

          Google DeepMind’s Alexander Lerchner just dropped a paper that cuts through the AI consciousness hype with surgical precision.

          His claim? LLMs will never be conscious. Not in 10 years. Not in 100. Not ever.

          And his argument isn’t biological chauvinism. It’s physics.

          The core idea: computation isn’t something that exists “out there” in matter. It requires what Lerchner calls a mapmaker. An experiencing agent who actively alphabetizes continuous physics into discrete symbols.

          Without that mapmaker, there’s no computation. Just electrons moving.

          His knockout line:
          “Expecting an algorithmic description to instantiate the quality it maps is like expecting the mathematical formula of gravity to physically exert weight.”

          3 takeaways:
          → Simulation ≠ Instantiation. A GPU modeling photosynthesis produces zero glucose.
          → Scaling compute won’t cross the causality gap. It’s structural, not quantitative.
          → AGI = powerful tool, not moral patient. Welfare concerns are misplaced.

          The map is not the territory. Are we finally ready to admit it?

          Link to the paper in the comments.

          #ai #research

          360 reactions360

          You don’t need more content, You need a voice people remember. And, I build that with you. My clients include Fortune 500’s, Govt of India & Govt of USA. Storyteller I Strategist I Speaker ++

          View my services

          9h •

          I wrote my first book in a parking lot. Yes, you read that correctly. A parking lot outside my kids' activity class. MacBook on my lap. Thums Up on the dashboard. Two hours every day. That was all I had.   I didn't have a VA who cleared the calendar. I didn't have a dedicated time or place. I just started. In the margins of a full life.   That book went on to be nominated at the Valley of Words Literature Festival. And, it became a finalist at the International Book Awards, USA.   And I remember thinking: If I can build something real in a parking lot, what could I build if I actually showed up with intention?   That question became Authique. That question became every stage I have stood on. That question became the work I now do with founders, CXOs, and leaders across the world.   I help them find the one thing they have been saying in private for years. And finally say it out loud.   In rooms that matter. To people who are ready to listen.   P.S. What is the one thing you have been waiting for the right moment to say?

            Ja ću to reći jednostavnije: Ćevapi su pobijedili pizzu. 😄 I ne bilo kakvi ćevapi. Ćevapi sa lukom i kajmakom. Oni ozbiljni. Oni koji ne traže dopuštenje da budu bolji. Neki će danas analizirati taktiku, posjed i penale. Italija je imala ime. Bosna je imala srce. Italija je donijela pizzu. Bosna je donijela glad. I na kraju… na Svjetsko prvenstvo u Amerike ne ide pizza. Id u ćevapi. Sa lukom. Sa kajmakom. I sa cijelim Balkanom u grudima. 🇧🇦🔥 Neka se zna: nekad ne pobijedi favorit. Nekad pobijedi ono što ima dušu. Bravo Bosno. Idemo u "Amerike." ✊️

            Ajdin Salihović likes this

            Dr. Vimal Rawat

            • 2nd

            Division AI Lead at BASF.

            22h •

            It’s funny because it is true 😂

            John Wernfeldt

            • 2nd

            I help CDOs stop firefighting data problems and start leading with strategic authority | Ex-Gartner

            Visit my website

            1d •

            I sat through a meeting like this last year.

            Seven people in the room deciding the AI strategy.

            The Chief AI Officer had read the Gartner report. The VP of Digital saw something at Davos. The Head of Innovation had built a GPT wrapper. Two strategy consultants had the slide deck. The vendor was already selling the solution.

            The data engineer wasn’t invited.

            She found out about the new strategy the same way everyone else did.

            Except she was the one expected to deliver it.

            On data she knew was broken. With definitions nobody had agreed on. And a pipeline that hadn’t been documented since 2021.

            I keep seeing this pattern.

            The people deciding the AI strategy have never looked at the data. They’ve read reports about it. Seen demos of it. Built slides about it.

            But never actually opened a table and checked whether the numbers make sense.

            Then they announce the plan.

            And the person who actually knows the system spends the next six months explaining why it won’t work as designed.

            The people who know the least decide the most.

            If you want your AI strategy to survive contact with reality:

            → Invite the data team before the strategy is announced
            → Ask them what’s broken before you promise what’s possible
            → Let the people who build the system shape what gets built on top of it

            The slide deck doesn’t break when you deploy it.

            The data does.

            I wrote a free playbook on fixing the foundations in 30 days:             https://lnkd.in/dGDjTkev

            ➤ Follow John for daily posts on what actually breaks in data teams and how to fix it.
            🔔 Tap the bell on my profile to get notified when I post.
            ♻️ Repost if your data team finds out about strategy the same way everyone else does.

              Feed post

              Government Digital Service

              7h •

              More than 150 public sector engineers joined us for a one-day hackathon where we explored how AI coding tools can help engineers to solve real problems. 🧩    
               
              The event took place as part of AI Engineering Lab – an initiative led by the GDS Engineering Excellence team, designed to accelerate the effective and responsible use of AI coding assistants across software engineering teams in the public sector.

              Participants spent a focused day together working on a brief relevant to their work and learning from each other about how to make the most of AI coding tools. Each team created a working prototype with support from their AI tools and judges decided on a winning team by asking questions about what they built, how they used AI tools and what they plan to do next.

              Congratulations to the Data & Insights Team from National Infrastructure and Service Transformation Authority (NISTA) who won first place for their CUB project management platform!

              A huge thank you to everyone who joined us in London last week for a productive day.

              🔗 Read more: https://lnkd.in/eKngvYbN

              #DigitalGovernment #PublicSectorInnovation #DigitalTransformation #AI #GovTech #ArtificialIntelligence #GovernmentDigitalAndData

              Join AI Engineering Lab and  help shape the future of digital government Join AI Engineering Lab and  help shape the future of digital government 

              cddo.blog.gov.uk

              Jasmin Alić

              • Following

              Award-winning personal branding & business coach | Founder of Link Up community (in 70+ countries) | Teaching you how to build a trusted online brand & profitable business

              Visit my website

              6d • Edited •

              LinkedIn gave you a platform to impact people's lives. Beyond just business. So let me say this:

              Double down on “what always works”.

              1. If you're just getting started, never quit. Keep posting relentlessly. Keep building endlessly. Lara Acosta did it. And she's now made it to Forbes 30 under 30!

              (she just called me with the news, go and support the flock out of her post today!!!)

              Proud of you, Lara. 🏆

              2. If you're not getting leads consistently, get in front of more people. Whether via the comments, via DMs, or via live events. Collaborate. Fail. Talk. Sell. Do it all.

              Get your name out there. No one else will.

              3. Find your tribe in the process. Support others and you'll be supported. It's the exact reason I built my Link Up community, so you have a place to "belong".

              This journey may be lonely. But the top isn't.

              Let's all strive to the top together, eh?

              Let's use this public stage for good.

              Coach J is rooting for you all. ❤️

              (Repost to motivate others ♻️)

              P.S. Ask me anything in the comments! I'll respond to at least 200+ of you. I love helping in the comments.

              Feed post

              Nabeela Irfan commented

              Richard Moore

              • 2nd

              I train founders in the psychology of high-trust deal-making and sales | Creator, Art of Sell | 3× LinkedIn Top Voice | 5 x LinkedIn Learning Instructor | Incoming Author with Wiley in 2027: Building Authority

              Visit my website

              4h • Edited •

              One thing I’ll never do? Insult you by saying:


              "I’ll help you get to six-figures"

              Because if six figures excites you,
              I'm probably not for you
              And that's fine

              For most of my clients, $100k is probably a pay cut
              ↳ They've run teams
              ↳ Led divisions
              ↳ Closed deals worth more than most people's annual revenue

              And now they're solo

              Which means their problem now is that nobody teaches conversion at the level they're used to operating at

              - Every course out there is calibrated for someone making their first $10k
              - And not for someone who used to earn $250k and is trying to build something that reflects what they're actually worth

              (An ex-global director from Google recently closed her first $25k through my LinkedIn Client Accelerator)

              - I'm sharing this because she had plenty of knowledge
              - What she was missing was someone who understood the level
              - And could show her how to sell at that level without a brand behind her.

              It's what I keep seeing

              And it's the reason I built both Art of Sell and my Accelerator the way I did

              ↳ Speakers who've led global teams at Nike, Coca-Cola, Goldman Sachs and Accenture
              ↳ Sessions built for people who've been in serious rooms and need to feel like they're still in one
              ↳ And a standard of conversation that doesn't waste your time with basics you learnt 15 years ago

              I built this for the kind of person who's been overlooked by an industry obsessed with capturing beginners through hype.

              --
              PS If you want to see the level we operate at, Art of Sell is free for 7 days.
              Join us here: https://lnkd.in/eHw5fitm

              Or DM me directly about the Accelerator where over 250 ex-corporate solo founders have joined.

              Aishah Sofea bin Nor Shafiq, a 4-year-old "Little Dreamer" from Malaysia Federation Of The Deaf (MFD), imagined a world of gnomes and candy houses. Using Qwen 3.6-Plus and Wan 2.7, Alibaba Cloud transformed her clay-inspired art into a high-fidelity animation.

              ✨ Qwen captured the poetic intent of her storytelling.
              ✨ Wan rendered her vision into a fluid, tactile reality.

              This is #AIforGood in action—turning imagination into an inclusive, multisensory experience.

                Feed post

                Harry Stebbings

                • Following

                Founder @ 20VC

                22h •

                Zapp is one of the most untold success stories in European technology.

                In a world where everyone burned through $100Ms of venture dollars and died, they kept their heads down and survived as the winner.

                Three core lessons from Zapp's success when everyone else in that category failed:

                1. Focus on ICP (ideal customer profile). They did not launch everywhere. They focused on specific areas with specific products at specific prices, targeting their customers on specific channels. They deeply knew their customer, found their first thousand true fans, and did not go too broad too soon.

                2. Capital efficiency. They did not spend on ridiculous sports teams, marketing campaigns, or lavish expenses, which ruin capital efficiency. They appreciated that margin superiority defined longevity and that longevity in this business is success.

                3. Fundraising discipline. They raised what they needed to. Not what they could. Their largest competitor raised 6x more capital than them. The discipline they had on fundraising ensured that their preference stack was low. Limitations reveal style. Zapp have shown their style over the last few years.

                Founders; focus. Row your own race. Do not get swept up in what others are doing. Know the metrics that matter. Execute to them. Earn the right to do the next thing.

                #founder #funding #business #investing #vc #venturecapital #entrepreneur #startup

                22h

                The ICP point is the one most founders intellectually agree with and emotionally resist. Going narrow feels like giving up, but it's usually the only way to build something people actually love before you scale

                Feed post

                Paul Willson finds this insightful

                Jonathan Farrow

                • 2nd

                Founder Minns.ai

                3h • Edited •

                Should you use the latest model and is it economical for you or the providers?

                Toby Ord just published an analysis of METR's benchmark data against hourly cost

                At each model's cheapest operating point:
                • Sonnet 3.5 / Grok 4: ~$0.40/hr
                • GPT-5 on ~45-min tasks: ~$13/hr
                • o3 at its sweet spot: ~$40/hr
                • Human software engineer: ~$120/hr

                When a model is pushed to its full advertised time horizon, costs climb 10–100x. o3 at its 1.5-hour horizon is roughly $350/hr more than a human and still fails half the time.

                Why this matters if you're building with agents:

                1. METR is measuring peak capability, not economic capability. Part of that curve is driven by lavish spending on inference.

                2. There's mounting evidence that hourly costs are themselves growing exponentially with the time horizon. If that holds, the gap between "what's possible" and "what's deployable" widens every generation.

                3. The sweet spot where marginal returns start diminishing is the number that matters for production workloads. Most public benchmarks don't highlight it.

                For anyone architecting agent systems today, the takeaway is to design for the sweet spot, not the plateau. And to treat headline time-horizon forecasts as an upper bound on what's technically feasible, not a timeline for what's economically viable.

                What will become more important is the scaffolding around agents and the memory layer to reduce inference cost and improve reliability

                Check out https://minns.ai/ for next-generation agent memory and data infrastructure

                #AIAgents #LLMs #AIInfrastructure

                Feed post

                James Riley

                • 3rd+

                Account Director - Verint EMEA

                Promoted

                “AI Isn’t Magic, It’s Just Very Fancy Maths” At least once a week, I hear someone whisper, “Our AI project failed.” Not with sadness. More like the tone people use when they say, “We accidentally summoned a demon.” There’s fear. There’s confusion. There’s usually a slide deck. The truth? AI isn’t mysterious. It isn’t emotional. And it definitely doesn’t wake up one morning, stretch its metaphorical limbs, and think, “Today I’m automating Helen from Billing.” AI doesn’t have ambition, doesn’t hold grudges and doesn’t “decide” things the way humans mean it. It’s algorithms. Inputs, patterns, probabilities. That’s it! Feed it good information, the right context, a clearly designed outcome and it does exactly what you asked. Feed it rubbish, and it will confidently produce very polished rubbish and look you straight in the eye while doing it. Most “failed AI projects” didn’t fail because the technology wasn’t powerful enough. They failed because AI was treated like a magical intern instead of an extremely literal machine. No clear objective. No operational context. No plan for when the output doesn’t quite match reality. AI doesn’t understand “your business”. It understands data that represents your business, often imperfectly, like explaining an internal joke to someone who wasn’t there and waiting for the laugh. Which brings us to the unglamorous bit nobody puts on the keynote slide: Data is king. AI runs on data like cars run on fuel. And like fuel, quality matters. You can have the most impressive engine in the world, but if the data’s old, fragmented, or inconsistent, you’re not going anywhere fast. That’s why the organisations moving fastest with AI rarely start from zero, they already have data in place, or partners who’ve been building it for years. We’re talking real customer interactions. Years of repeated behaviours, edge cases & awkward exceptions. The stuff that never survives a neat process diagram but happens every single day. And that data, more than any model or algorithm, quietly decides whether AI actually works. That operational memory becomes fuel for AI. Not magic… fuel. Just well‑labelled, painfully realistic inputs. Which is why the smartest AI journeys don’t start with, “What can the model do?” but with, “What problem are we actually solving and what data do we trust?” Magic is for Disney+. AI is plumbing. Nobody admires plumbing when it works. They notice it when everything floods. But get the pipes right, clean data in, sensible logic throughout, clear guardrails at the edges and life gets easier. Less chaos. Faster outcomes. Fewer hushed conversations about robots taking over billing. So no, your AI project didn’t fail because the machine had a mind of its own. It failed because the inputs, the design or expectations were broken. Fix those, and AI becomes what it was always meant to be: Not magic. Not scary. Just very fancy maths quietly doing its job. And honestly? That’s way more impressive anyway.

                59 reactions59

                Checkout Boost

                3w •

                “Global logistics disruptions may impact delivery schedules…”

                This small message does a lot of heavy lifting.

                Simple. Clear. Honest.

                No over-promising. No hiding behind vague timelines.

                And that’s exactly why this kind of messaging is becoming more common.

                At checkout, customers aren’t just deciding what to buy — they’re deciding whether to trust you.
                A delay without context feels like a broken promise.
                A delay with transparency feels understandable.

                Brands that proactively communicate:
                • Reduce support tickets
                • Build trust
                • Set the right expectations
                • Protect post-purchase experience

                This is especially critical today, with global shipping still being unpredictable.

                Optionally display message only for international orders, without confusing local shoppers.

                Checkout isn’t just about conversion anymore.
                It’s about confidence.

                Sometimes, a single line of honest copy can do more than any discount ever could.

                Set it up in minutes—no coding required.

                TOOKLE

                8h •

                New listings in TOOKLE!

                Two founders. Different worlds. Same obsession.
                APIPosture is building the clarity layer for API security. Every endpoint, every secret, every OWASP exposure, mapped in 2 minutes, 100% local. No agent. No cloud leak. Just a live picture of your attack surface, on every deploy.

                EventGraph, Ajay Prashanth is building the clarity layer for prediction markets. Probabilities, liquidity, pricing, pulled from every platform into a single, actionable feed. What used to take 20 tabs and a spreadsheet now takes one query.

                Both founders stared at a mess the industry had learned to tolerate, sprawling APIs, splintered prediction markets, and refused to accept that "fragmented by default" was good enough. They built the lens.

                We genuinely want them to win. Not because they're on Tookle. Because founders who build clarity layers, people willing to spend months inside other people's mess, deserve to be seen. Not three rounds late. Not after the cycle closes on them. Now.

                If their work speaks to you, say hello. Follow their build. Tell them what you see.

                  Executive Editor at Bug, Tech Influencer, Moderator, Speaker and Mobile Devices Expert Group Manager at EISA

                  9h •

                  Posljednjih nekoliko mjeseci radio sam na nečemu velikom. Danas to konačno mogu podijeliti.

                  Zajedno s Katarinom Zubatović kreirao sam "Sve AI tajne" – sveobuhvatnu edukaciju za automatizaciju, skaliranje i boostanje biznisa kroz upoznavanje i upotrebu niza najnaprednijih AI alata.

                  Ovo nije tečaj o tome što je AI. Ovo je implementacija AI agenata u praksi koji postaju vaši softverski radnici, s radnim vremenom od 0 do 24.

                  Svaki polaznik prolazi kroz individualizirani onboarding prije nego edukacija uopće počne. Dobivaju unaprijed personalizirani AI Roadmap i AI predloške za svoj tip biznisa. Po završetku odlaze s kompletnim sustavom AI agenata koji rade za njih, doslovce dok spavaju ili piju kavu.

                  Uči se, između ostalog, i ovo:
                  - ChatGPT Mastery + Custom GPTs
                  - Claude Projects za strategijski rad
                  - Agentic AI – izgradnja agenata korak po korak
                  - Automatizacija bez kodiranja (Zapier, Make, Apollo)
                  - AI za content, marketing i video

                  Prije 30 godina, tko nije naučio raditi u Excelu i Wordu – zaostao je. Danas je ista stvar s AI alatima. Razlika je samo u tome što ovaj put promjena dolazi 10 puta brže.

                  Format: cjelodnevna live radionica u Zagrebu (19. svibnja), pa niz video lekcija s doživotnim pristupom plus podrška kroz zatvorenu Slack zajednicu.

                  Broj polaznika je strogo ograničen – 30 mjesta ukupno, a prijave, kompletan kurikulum i svi detalji su ovdje: https://lnkd.in/ee-sj9Vt

                  AI nije taj koji će uzeti posao ljudima. Ljudi koji znaju koristiti AI uzet će posao onima koji ne znaju.

                  Feed post

                  New comment in your group

                  Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Data Science & Robotics

                  Dibya Jyoti Datta • 3rd+

                  1w •

                  Most people are watching intelligence.
                  The real story is behaviour.

                  And almost no one is ready for that shift.

                  We’ve been sold a simple idea.
                  Smarter models = better results = fewer jobs.

                  That story breaks the moment you see agentic AI in action.

                  Because now AI doesn’t just answer.
                  It plans.
                  It acts.
                  It remembers.
                  It retries.

                  And suddenly… it behaves.

                  Here’s the shift most people miss:

                  → Models are getting better
                  → But models are not the edge anymore
                  → Systems are the edge

                  Anyone can access strong models today.
                  Few know how to manage what those models actually do over time.

                  That’s where things get messy.

                  Because once AI starts operating, new problems show up:
                  1. It forgets context when you need it most
                  2. It gives confident but wrong outputs
                  3. It loops, retries, and burns cost silently

                  Simple demo systems look perfect.
                  Real systems break in quiet ways.

                  That’s the gap.

                  The winners won’t be the ones with the smartest model.
                  They’ll be the ones who control behaviour.

                  So what actually matters now?

                  → Memory that improves outcomes over time

                  → Feedback loops that correct mistakes fast

                  → Guardrails that stop bad actions early

                  → Cost control before scale kills margins

                  → Clear failure handling when things go wrong

                  This is not shiny work.
                  But this is where trust is built.

                  And trust is what turns AI into real leverage.

                  So ask better questions:

                  What happens when it fails?

                  What happens when it forgets?

                  What happens when it is confidently wrong?

                  If you can answer that,
                  you’re not just using AI anymore.

                  You’re managing it.

                  I share my learning journey here. Join me and let’s grow together.

                  Enjoy this? Repost it to your network and follow Dibya Jyoti Datta for more

                  1,968 reactions1,968

                  21 comments21 comments

                  •

                  239 reposts239 reposts

                  Send

                  Prashanth Reddy Sripuram Verified Profile 3rd+Prashanth Reddy Sripuram • 3rd+

                  Delivery Manager | Atlassian Cloud Migration & Integration | Jira, JSM, Confluence | Governance & AI-enabled Service Delivery

                  1w

                  Strong perspective on systems being the edge. But what’s often called “AI forgetting” is really context drift and unmanaged branching. The real leverage comes from knowing when to intervene — reframing prompts, resetting context, and applying human governance at the right moment. That’s less about using AI… and more about operating it effectively.
